Fnrn Polrrrcal PnacrrcEs CouurssroN
428 I Street . Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916t 322-5660 o Fax (916\ 322-0886
August3,2006
David L. Berry, Ph.D.
President, CSWD
Caspar South Water District
P.O.Box744
Mendocino, CA 95460
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-06-144
Dear Dr. Berry:
This letter is in response to your request for advice, on behalf of yourself and your
fellow board members, regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").' The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to
any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized
representative. (Section 831 1a@); regulation 18329(a), copy enclosed.) However, the
Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or
otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(bX8XF).)
Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by
the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section
83114; regulation I8329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we
decline to provide written advice to you at this time. Your current question involves an
analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred.
Issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further
the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 831 1 1.
Moreover, the Commission must decline to give advice at this time, as the same or very
similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement
Division.
Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your
request. In addition, nothing in the Act prevents you, or the other board members, from
seeking legal advice from private counsel.
t Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear atTitle 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.


File No. W-06-144
Page No. 2
If you have other questions on this matter,please contact me at (916) 322_5660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor sought advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act applicable to board members of Caspar South Water District.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the analysis would relate to past conduct and would not further effective administration of the Act. Additionally, the same or similar issues are the subject of a pending Enforcement Division investigation.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is a declination letter (File No. W-06-144). The document lacks explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections but contains the substantive reasoning for the declination. OCR quality appears good with no significant parsing issues detected.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor sought advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act applicable to board members of Caspar South Water District.

CONCLUSION: After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. Your current question involves an analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred. Issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111. Moreover, the Commission must decline to give advice at this time, as the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide formal written advice to David L. Berry, Ph.D., and fellow Caspar South Water District board members regarding Political Reform Act conflict-of-interest provisions, citing that the request involves analysis of past conduct and overlaps with an ongoing Enforcement Division investigation.
